shots.label: pdp-bpc
shots.title: BPC-157 Reference Standard | Cytoprotective Peptide
shots.text:
Search for products
All
EN
Log in
0 items in cart, view bag
Home
Cytoprotective Peptides
BPC-157
BPC-157
Cytoprotective Peptides

>98% HPLC

HPLC PURITY

Certificate of Analysis

VIEW PDF

Ships in 24h

COLD-CHAIN

Reconstitution

OPEN CALCULATOR

$70.00
Quantity
1
Add to bag
Secure checkout
Free delivery over $150

For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. All products are sold strictly for in-vitro research and laboratory use.

Description

BPC-157 is a synthetic pentadecapeptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, corresponding to a fragment of a protection protein originally isolated from human gastric juice. It has the empirical formula C62H98N16O22 and a molecular weight of approximately 1419.5 Da. The peptide is proline-rich and classified as a cytoprotective reference compound in preclinical literature.

BPC-157 has been used as a reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo studies of endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) signalling, angiogenic responses, and Src–Caveolin‑1–eNOS pathway modulation in vascular tissues. It is also employed in cell-based assays exploring cytoprotection and stress-response pathways in gastrointestinal and musculoskeletal models.

Supplied as an HPLC-verified lyophilized powder at >98% purity for research use. Typically reconstituted in sterile water or buffered aqueous media for in‑vitro applications, and stored lyophilized at 2–8°C, with aliquots maintained at −20°C after reconstitution, protected from light and moisture. Reference standard for in-vitro research on cytoprotective and angiogenic signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

Product Details
Purity
>98% HPLC
Form
Lyophilized powder
Storage
2–8°C lyophilized; -20°C reconstituted; protect from light
Molecular Weight
1419.5 Da
CAS Number
137525-51-0
Sequence
GEPPPGKPADDAGLV
Origin
Synthetic
Solubility
Water, buffered aqueous solutions
Research Category
Cytoprotective peptide
Frequently Asked Questions
What is the amino acid sequence and length of BPC-157?
BPC-157 is a 15-amino-acid peptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, derived from a larger gastric protection protein.
Which in-vitro pathways is BPC-157 commonly used to study?
It is frequently used in vascular and cell-culture models investigating eNOS activation, VEGF/VEGFR2-related angiogenic signalling, and cytoprotective stress-response pathways.
Is the identity of BPC-157 supported by public chemical databases?
Yes. BPC-157 and its salt forms are listed in PubChem (e.g., CID 78169625 and CID 9941957) with standardized structural and physicochemical data.
Research References
PubChem CID 78169625. BPC 157 trifluoroacetate. National Center for Biotechnology Information, 2024.
Wu C-H et al., 2020. Modulatory effects of BPC 157 on vasomotor tone and Src–Caveolin‑1–eNOS signalling in rat aorta. Scientific Reports.
Sikiric P. et al., 2024. Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 pleiotropic activity review. Inflammopharmacology.
Shipping & Returns

Free shipping on orders over $150. Standard delivery 3-7 business days.

All products are shipped in temperature-controlled packaging to maintain stability.

Returns accepted within 14 days of delivery for unopened, sealed items only. Contact support for return authorization.

USA premium research peptides and biotech compounds. Every product is tested and verified by trusted US labs — purity (HPLC and MS), endotoxins, heavy metals, microbial safety, and spectral analysis — to guarantee the integrity of your research results.

shots.title: Peptide Reconstitution Calculator
shots.text:
Search for products
All
EN

0 items in cart, view bag
RESEARCH TOOL
Peptide Reconstitution Calculator

Enter vial mass, diluent volume, and target dose to calculate the exact draw volume and syringe units for in-vitro reconstitution protocols.

For Research Use Only. This calculator supports in-vitro reconstitution protocols for research-grade reference compounds. It is not medical advice and must not be used to prepare material for human or animal administration. Always follow applicable laws and institutional research protocols.

Calculator
Worked Example
Handling Guide
FAQ
History1
Inputs
Peptide Amount
mg
mcg
2 mg
5 mg
10 mg
15 mg
30 mg
BAC Water Volume
mL
1 mL
2 mL
3 mL
Desired Dose
mcg
mg
Syringe Type

U-100 insulin syringes. 1 unit = 0.01 mL.

0.3 mL
0.5 mL
1 mL
5 mg
2 mL BAC water
250 mcg dose
1 mL (U-100)
Results
Concentration
2.50 mg/mL2500 mcg/mL
Draw Volume
0.10 mL
Syringe Units (U-100)
10 units
Doses per Vial
20 doses
Show exact values

INSTRUCTIONS

Step 1: Add 2 mL of bacteriostatic water to the 5 mg vial. Swirl gently until dissolved. Step 2: Using a 1 mL (U-100) insulin syringe, draw to the 10-unit mark (0.10 mL) for a 250 mcg dose.

Copy Result
Share Result
How is this calculated?
All inputs are normalized to base units before calculation: peptide mass to mg, dose to mg, volume to mL. If you enter a dose in mcg, the engine divides by 1,000 to convert to mg first.

Concentration = Peptide Amount (mg) / Water Volume (mL)

Draw Volume = Desired Dose (mg) / Concentration (mg/mL)

Syringe Units = Draw Volume (mL) x 100(U-100 standard: 100 units = 1 mL, so 1 unit = 0.01 mL)

Doses per Vial = Total Peptide (mg) / Dose Amount (mg)

INSTRUCTIONS

Step 1: Add 2 mL of bacteriostatic water to the 5 mg vial. Swirl gently until dissolved. Step 2: Using a 1 mL (U-100) insulin syringe, draw to the 10-unit mark (0.10 mL) for a 250 mcg dose.

Copy Result

How is this calculated?
All inputs are normalized to base units before calculation: peptide mass to mg, dose to mg, volume to mL. If you enter a dose in mcg, the engine divides by 1,000 to convert to mg first.

Concentration = Peptide Amount (mg) / Water Volume (mL)

Draw Volume = Desired Dose (mg) / Concentration (mg/mL)

Syringe Units = Draw Volume (mL) x 100(U-100 standard: 100 units = 1 mL, so 1 unit = 0.01 mL)

Doses per Vial = Total Peptide (mg) / Dose Amount (mg)
